response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but his experience is almost entirely in web, mobile, and product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang web services). The role requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his background. His low-latency systems work and C/C++ exposure are tangential at best, and there is no deep-learning workload or hardware architecture experience to build on for a Principal-level performance architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, exceeding the raw years-of-experience bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Some C/C++ and systems-level exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components, audio/wave-field-synthesis thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from Principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU performance expertise
Strong general engineering tenure with some low-latency systems work
